children.heading: Adoptive placements; preferences
children.label: a Adoptive placements; preferences
children.text: In any adoptive placement of an Indian child under State law, a preference shall be given, in the absence of good cause to the contrary, to a placement with (1) a member of the child’s extended family; (2) other members of the Indian child’s tribe; or (3) other Indian families.

children.heading: Foster care or preadoptive placements; criteria; preferences
children.label: b Foster care or preadoptive placements; criteria; preferences
i
children.text: a member of the Indian child’s extended family;

children.label: ii
children.text: a foster home licensed, approved, or specified by the Indian child’s tribe;

children.label: iii
children.text: an Indian foster home licensed or approved by an authorized non-Indian licensing authority; or

children.label: iv
children.text: an institution for children approved by an Indian tribe or operated by an Indian organization which has a program suitable to meet the Indian child’s needs.

children.heading: Tribal resolution for different order of preference; personal preference considered; anonymity in application of preferences
children.label: c Tribal resolution for different order of preference; personal preference considered; anonymity in application of preferences
children.text: In the case of a placement under subsection (a) or (b) of this section, if the Indian child’s tribe shall establish a different order of preference by resolution, the agency or court effecting the placement shall follow such order so long as the placement is the least restrictive setting appropriate to the particular needs of the child, as provided in subsection (b) of this section. Where appropriate, the preference of the Indian child or parent shall be considered: , That where a consenting parent evidences a desire for anonymity, the court or agency shall give weight to such desire in applying the preferences.Provided

children.heading: Social and cultural standards applicable
children.label: d Social and cultural standards applicable
children.text: The standards to be applied in meeting the preference requirements of this section shall be the prevailing social and cultural standards of the Indian community in which the parent or extended family resides or with which the parent or extended family members maintain social and cultural ties.

children.heading: Record of placement; availability
children.label: e Record of placement; availability
children.text: A record of each such placement, under State law, of an Indian child shall be maintained by the State in which the placement was made, evidencing the efforts to comply with the order of preference specified in this section. Such record shall be made available at any time upon the request of the Secretary or the Indian child’s tribe.
